publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-03-13 23:58:16 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, pictures, diaries, and other papers.
did.abstract: Papers include letters written to the Kramer family from Kevin Kramer while in Vienna. Also, pictures, letters, and diaries relating to Jeffrey A. Kramer's service aboard the U.S.S. Nimitz; papers concerning Samuel Kramer's World War II service; and Anne Gudis' and Samuel's war-time correspondence. Also, papers, research materials, notes, clippings, and correspondence relating to the history of the Ithaca Jewish Community.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: Date completed:
defitem.item: Sept. 1986; rev. May 1991; rev. Oct. 1991; addl. Oct. 1993; addl. June 1995

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Kramer family papers, #3970. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Samuel Kramer of Ithaca, New York married Anne Gudis of Newark, New Jersey. Sam served in the United States Army during World War II. In 2009, he was recognized for his wartime service by being named a Chevalier of the Legion of Honor by the French government. Sam and Anne had two sons, Kevin and Jeffrey, and a daughter, Michele. Kevin attended Cornell University and the Institute of European Studies in Vienna, Austria. Jeffrey earned a Master.s Degree in Hospital Administration from Cornell University in 1973. He served aboard the U.S.S. Nimitz at the time of the aborted rescue mission of the U.S. hostages in Iran in 1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Family papers comprise correspondence and greeting cards, including letters written to the Kramer family from Kevin Kramer while in Vienna; pictures, letters, and diaries relating to Jeffrey A. Kramer's service aboard the U.S.S. Nimitz; papers concerning Samuel Kramer's World War II service and his selection to the French Legion of Honor, including his 2009 certificate from the Legion; and Anne Gudis' and Samuel's war-time correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also, papers, research materials, notes, clippings, and correspondence relating to the history of the Ithaca Jewish community.

did.unittitle: Letters to Anne Gudis from Samuel Kramer
Letters; also, photograph (possibly of Anne Gudis)
Letter to Samuel Kramer from Anne Gudis
Letters to Anne Gudis from various persons in response to her letter to Samuel Kramer, published in YANK,
Anne Krammer's notes on the Ithaca Jewish Community
"High above Cayuga's Waters - Jewish Life in Ithaca, NY" by Anne Kramer
Ithaca Jewish History – writings by Anne Kramer
Correspondence: Jeff Kramer and Gene Dannen
Anne Kramer's notes: the Ithaca Jewish Community
Autobiography of Sam Kuntz, Ithaca restauranteur - his son founded Hal's Deli
Father's diary of trip to Israel
Miscellany: letters, cards, 1887 unidentified photo, data on Morris Lubliner
Papers re: Sam Kramer's military service in World War II
Papers re: Jewish War Veterans of World War II
Photographs (4) of Sam Kramer and other Jewish War Veterans
Personal papers of Sam and Anne Kramer
Letters of Kevin Kramer from the Institute of European Studies in Vienna, Austria
Letters of Kevin Kramer from the John Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies in Bologna, Italy
Handbooks and newsgrams re: the U.S.S. Nimitz
Photographs of Yon Kippur services on the U.S.S. Nimitz
Photographs of Kramer house, Oak Avenue, Ithaca
Tape recordings (4) sent to Anne and Sam Kramer by Jeffery
Tape recording: reminiscences by Sam Kramer
Greeting cards and thank you notes
Unidentified children and adults (possibly their teachers)
Notes on Jews, Hillel interview, article re Assunta Sullivan
Notes on Jews in American history
Jewish publications relating to history and genealogy
Publications including 50th anniversary of Temple Beth-El [booklet] and article re Stephen S. Wise
High Above Cayuga's Waters, Alpha Bits
Temple Beth-El Bulletin, other published items, Wall of Liberty Certificate
Correspondences for the Jews in Small Towns Project
Copies of military records and clippings relating to his military service during World War II
Samuel Kramer's certificate from the "Ordre National de la Legion D'Honneur"
